name: Chocolate-Pumpkin Layer Cake with Brown-Sugar Frosting
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Butter or margarine, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Timing Tip: Can be stored at room temperature up to 5 days.
Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly grease two 9-inch round cake pans. Line the bottoms with waxed paper.
2. Mix flour, cocoa powder, pumpkin-pie spice, baking powder, and baking soda in a bowl.
3. In a large bowl, beat butter and granulated sugar until pale and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time until the mixture resembles mayonnaise.
4. With the mixer on low speed, beat in pumpkin, milk, and vanilla. The mixture will look curdled.
5. Gradually beat in the flour mixture, scraping the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula 2 or 3 times.
6. Divide batter between prepared pans and smooth the tops.
7. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes, or until a pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Let cakes cool in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes. Run a knife around the edges of the cakes to loosen them, then invert them onto wire racks. Remove the pans and waxed paper. Allow cakes to cool completely.
Frosting:
9. In a medium saucepan, combine brown sugar, butter, and milk. Bring to a gentle boil and stir until the butter melts, the sugar dissolves, and the mixture is smooth.
10. Using a hand-held electric mixer, gradually beat confectioners' sugar and vanilla into the hot mixture. Alternatively, pour the hot mixture into a bowl and beat in the sugar and vanilla with a stand mixer.
11. Immediately place one cake layer upside-down on a serving plate. Spread with warm frosting (if the frosting cools, it may set and form a crust on the surface; beat again until smooth).
12. Place the other cake layer right side up over the sides and top. Cover with plastic wrap or store in a cake keeper.
Serves 12. Per serving: 824 calories, 7 grams protein, 128 grams carbohydrates, 34 grams fat, 156 milligrams cholesterol (with butter), 76 milligrams cholesterol (with margarine), 496 milligrams sodium.
